items.name: WebSocket
items.description: Built-in RFC 6455 WebSocket server. Register handlers with app.ws(path, handler). Each connection receives a WebSocketConnection instance with send/receive, ping/pong, binary support, and event emitter methods. Configure max payload size, ping intervals, and client verification.

options.notes: Maximum incoming message size in bytes.
Automatic ping interval in ms (0 to disable).
Verification function: (req) => boolean. Return false to reject.

tips: ws.data is a plain object for storing per-connection user data (name, auth, etc.).
Check ws.readyState === 1 (OPEN) before sending to avoid errors.
Use verifyClient to enforce authentication before accepting the upgrade.
Properties: id, readyState, protocol, extensions, headers, ip, query, url, secure, connectedAt, uptime, bufferedAmount.

methods.description: Send a text or binary message.

methodReturns.description: Sends the data through the WebSocket connection.

methodParams.notes: String for text frames; Buffer for binary.

methodReturns.description: JSON-serializes obj and sends it as a text frame.

methodParams.notes: Value serialized to JSON and sent as a text frame.

methodReturns.description: Sends a ping control frame to the client.
Sends a pong control frame (usually in response to a ping).

methodParams.notes: Optional payload to include in the pong.

methods.description: Listen for events: message, close, error, ping, pong, drain.

methodReturns.description: Returns the WebSocket for chaining.

methodParams.notes: Callback invoked when the event fires.

methodReturns.description: Returns the WebSocket for chaining.

methodParams.notes: Callback invoked when the event fires.

methodReturns.description: Returns the WebSocket for chaining.

methodParams.notes: Callback invoked when the event fires.

methods.description: Graceful close with optional status code and reason.

methodReturns.description: Initiates a clean closing handshake.

methodParams.notes: Close code (e.g. `1000` = normal, `1001` = going away). Defaults to `1000`.

methodReturns.description: Forcibly destroys the socket without a handshake.

items.name: WebSocketPool
items.description: Connection and room manager for WebSocket apps. Automatically tracks connections, manages room membership, supports broadcast/targeted messaging, and cleans up on disconnect.

methods.description: Add a connection to the pool. Auto-removed on close.

methodReturns.description: Tracks the connection in the pool.

methods.description: Remove a connection from the pool and all rooms.

methodReturns.description: Removes the connection from the pool and all its rooms.

methods.description: Join a connection to a named room.

methodReturns.description: Adds ws to the named room.
Removes ws from the named room.

methods.description: Get all connections in a room.

methodReturns.description: Set of all connected WebSockets currently in the room.

methods.description: Get all rooms a connection is in.

methodReturns.description: Set of room names the given WebSocket has joined.

methodParams.notes: Connection whose room memberships to retrieve.

methodReturns.description: Sends the message to all connected clients (except excluded).

methodParams.notes: Message to send to all connections.

methodReturns.description: JSON-encodes obj and broadcasts to all clients.

methodParams.notes: Object to serialize as JSON and broadcast.

methods.description: Send to all in a room.

methodReturns.description: Sends msg to all clients in the named room.

methods.description: Send JSON to all in a room.

methodReturns.description: JSON-encodes obj and sends to all clients in the named room.
Total number of connected clients.
Live set of all connected WebSocket instances.
Map of all room names to their member sets.

methods.description: Number of connections in a room.

methodReturns.description: Number of clients currently in the named room.

methodParams.notes: Room name to count connections in.

methodReturns.description: Closes every tracked WebSocket connection.

items.name: SSE (Server-Sent Events)
items.description: Push real-time events to browser clients via res.sse(). Returns an SSEStream instance with auto-IDs, named events, keep-alive pings, and graceful disconnect handling. The browser connects with new EventSource(url).

options.notes: Keep-alive ping interval in ms (0 = disabled).
Pad the response with whitespace for proxy compatibility.

tips: SSE is automatically excluded from response compression.
Use keepAlive to prevent proxy/load-balancer timeouts on idle connections.
Properties: connected, eventCount, bytesSent, connectedAt, uptime, lastEventId, data (user store).

methodReturns.description: Returns the stream for chaining.

methodParams.notes: Text payload sent as a `data:` field.

methodReturns.description: Returns the stream for chaining.

methods.description: Send a named event. Browser listens with es.addEventListener(name, ...).

methodReturns.description: Returns the stream for chaining.

methodParams.notes: Event name. The client receives it as `event.type` in the `EventSource` handler.

methods.description: Send a comment line (not received by EventSource).

methodReturns.description: Returns the stream for chaining.

methodParams.notes: Comment text (`: text`). Keeps the connection alive without triggering client listeners.

methods.description: Set the reconnection interval for the client.

methodReturns.description: Returns the stream for chaining.

methodParams.notes: Reconnect delay sent to the client as a `retry:` field (milliseconds).

methodReturns.description: Starts auto-sending keep-alive comments at the given interval.

methodParams.notes: Interval in ms for sending `: ping` comments to prevent proxy timeouts.

methodReturns.description: Flushes the write buffer immediately.
Ends the SSE stream and closes the response.
Returns the stream for chaining.

methodParams.notes: Callback invoked when the event fires.
